properties.displayName: Suspicious Service Creation — Non-Standard Binary Path (T1543.003)
properties.description: Detects a Windows service installed with a binary path pointing to a suspicious executable — cmd.exe, powershell.exe, scripting engines, or binaries in user-writable paths. Adversaries use this technique (T1543.003) to establish persistence that survives reboots. Legitimate Windows services almost always run svchost.exe. Triage: check ServiceFileName, SubjectUserName, and what that account was doing in the 15 minutes prior. Tune the exclusion list for known-good third-party agents in your environment.
properties.query:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4697
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where ServiceFileName !startswith 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost'
| where ServiceFileName !startswith 'C:\\Windows\\system32\\svchost'
| where ServiceFileName !startswith 'C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\svchost'
| extend SuspiciousPath = ServiceFileName has_any (
    'cmd.exe',
    'powershell.exe',
    'pwsh.exe',
    'wscript.exe',
    'cscript.exe',
    'mshta.exe',
    'regsvr32.exe',
    'rundll32.exe',
    'AppData',
    '\\Temp\\',
    '\\temp\\',
    '\\Public\\',
    '\\Users\\'
  )
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    Computer,
    ServiceName,
    ServiceFileName,
    SubjectUserName,
    SubjectDomainName,
    SubjectLogonId,
    SuspiciousPath
| order by TimeGenerated desc
